Class FileUtils

java.lang.Object
FileUtils
public class FileUtils extends Object
Utility helpers for reading/writing playlists to CSV and for validating referenced audio files on disk. CSV format expected (with header row): Title,Artist,Duration,FilePath Duration is parsed as mm:ss by parseDurationToSeconds(..).

  • Constructor Details

    • FileUtils

      public FileUtils()

  • Method Details

    • savePlaylistToCSV

      public static void savePlaylistToCSV(String filename, DoublyLinkedSongList dlList)
      Save all songs from a linked list to a CSV file (overwrites if exists). Writes a header and then one row per song.

    • loadPlayListDLFromCSV

      public static DoublyLinkedSongList loadPlayListDLFromCSV(String fileName)
      Load a playlist from a CSV file into a new DoublyLinkedSongList. Skips the header line, reports malformed rows with line numbers, and continues parsing the rest.

    • parseDurationToSeconds

      public static int parseDurationToSeconds(String duration)
      Parse "mm:ss" into total seconds.
      Throws:
      NumberFormatException - if the format is not mm:ss or parts aren’t integers.

    • loadMultipleCSVsAndCombine

      public static int loadMultipleCSVsAndCombine(DoublyLinkedSongList dlList, Scanner input)
      Read multiple CSV paths (typed by user) and append all found songs into the provided linked list. Stops when the user types "Exit".
      Returns:
      total number of appended songs.

    • validateAudioFiles

      public static int validateAudioFiles(DoublyLinkedSongList dlList)
      Checks that every song’s absolute file path points to a real file. Prints a line for each missing/invalid file and returns how many were missing.
